author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
On the words of the Gospel, Matt. xix. 21,’Go, sell that thou hast, and give to the poor,’ etc.

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the words of the Gospel, Matt. xix. 21,’Go, sell that thou hast, and give to the poor,’ etc.

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Gospel by the present lesson has reminded me to speak to you, Beloved, of the heavenly treasure.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For our God hath not, as unbelieving covetous men suppose, wished us to lose what we have: if what hath been enjoined us be properly understood, and piously believed, and devoutly received; He hath not enjoined us to lose, but rather shown a place where we may lay up.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For no man can help thinking of his treasure, and following his riches in a kind of journeying of the heart.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If then they are buried in the earth, his heart will seek the lowest earth; but if they are reserved in heaven, his heart will be above.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If Christians therefore have the will to do what they know that they also make open profession of (not that all who hear know this; and I would that they who have known it, knew it not in vain); if then they have the will to “lift up the heart” above, let them lay up there, what they love; and though yet in the flesh on earth, let them dwell with Christ in heart; and as her Head went before the Church, so let the heart of the Christian go before him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As the members are to go where Christ the Head hath gone before, so shall each man at his rising again go where his heart hath now gone before.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us go hence then by that part of us which we may; our whole man will follow whither one part of us is gone before.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Our earthly house must fall to ruin; our heavenly house is eternal.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us move our goods beforehand, whither we are ourselves getting ready to come.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We have just heard a certain rich man seeking counsel from the “Good Master” as to the means of obtaining eternal life.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Great was the thing he loved, and of little value was that he was unwilling to renounce.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And so in perverseness of heart, on hearing Him whom he had but now called “Good Master,” through the overpowering love of what was valueless, he lost the possession of what was of great price.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If he had not wished to obtain eternal life, he would not have asked counsel how to obtain eternal life.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How is it then, Brethren, that he rejected the words of Him whom he had called “Good Master,” drawn out for him as they were from the doctrine of the faith?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Is He a Good Master before He teacheth, and when He hath taught, a bad one?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Before He taught, He was called “Good.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He did not hear what he wished, but he did hear what was proper for him; he had come with longing, but he went away in sadness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What if He had told him, “Lose what thou hast”? when he went away sad, because it was said, “Keep what thou hast securely.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Go,” saith He, “sell all that thou hast, and give to the poor.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Art thou afraid, it may be, lest thou shouldest lose it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See what follows; “And thou shall have treasure in heaven.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Before now it may be thou hast set some young slave to guard thy treasures; thy God will be the guardian of thy gold.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He who gave them on earth, will Himself keep them in heaven.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Perhaps he would not have hesitated to commit what he had to Christ, and was only sad because it was told him, “Give to the poor;” as though he would say in his heart, “Hadst Thou said, Give it to Me, I will keep it in heaven for thee; I would not hesitate to give it to my Lord, the ‘Good Master;’ but now thou hast said, ‘Give to the poor.’”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let no one fear to lay out upon the poor, let no one think that he is the receiver whose hand he sees.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He receives it Who bade thee give it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And this I say not out of mine own heart, or by any human conjecture; hear Him Himself, who at once exhorteth thee, and giveth thee a title of security.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “I was an hungred,” saith He, and ye gave Me meat.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And when after the enumeration of all their kind offices, they answered, “When saw we Thee an hungred?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He answered, “Inasmuch as ye have done it unto one of the least of these of Mine, ye have done it unto Me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is the poor man who begs, but He that is Rich receives.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou givest to one who will make away with it, He receiveth it Who will restore it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Nor will He restore only what He receiveth; He is pleased to borrow upon interest, He promiseth more than thou hast given.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Give the rein now to thy avarice, imagine thyself an usurer.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thou wert an usurer indeed, thou wouldest be rebuked by the Church, confuted by the word of God, all thy brethren would execrate thee, as a cruel usurer, desiring to wring gain from other’s tears.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But now be an usurer, no one will hinder thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou art willing to lend to a poor man, who whenever he may repay thee will do it with grief; but lend now to a debtor who is well able to pay, and who even exhorteth thee to receive what he promiseth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Give to God, and press God for payment.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yea rather give to God, and thou wilt be pressed to receive payment.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: On earth indeed thou hadst to seek thy debtor; and he sought too, but only to find where he might hide himself from thy face.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou hadst gone to the judge, and said, “Bid that my debtor be summoned;” and he on hearing this gets away, and cares not even to wish thee well, though to him perhaps in his need thou hadst given wealth by thy loan.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou hast one then on whom thou mayest well lay out thy money.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Give to Christ; He will of His own accord press thee to receive, whilst thou wilt even wonder that He hath received ought of thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For to them who are placed on His right hand He will first say, “Come, ye blessed of My Father.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Come” whither?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Receive the kingdom prepared for you from the foundation of the world.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “For I was an hundred, and ye gave Me meat; I was thirsty, and ye gave Me drink; I was a stranger, and ye took Me in; naked, and ye clothed Me; I was sick and in prison, and ye visited Me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And they will say, “Lord, when saw we Thee?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What doth this mean?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The debtor presses to pay, and the creditors make excuses.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But the trusty debtor will not let them suffer loss thereby.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Do ye hesitate to receive?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I have received, and are ye ignorant of it?” and He makes answer how He has received; “Inasmuch as ye have done it unto one of the least of these of Mine, ye have done it unto Me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “I received it not by Myself; but by Mine.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What was given to them came to Me; be secure, ye have not lost it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye looked to those who were little able to pay on earth; ye have One who is well able to pay in heaven.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I,” He saith, “have received, I will repay.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And what have I received, and what do I repay? “‘I was an hungred,’ He saith, ‘and ye gave Me meat;’ and the rest.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I received earth, I will give heaven; I received temporal things, I will restore eternal; I received bread, I will give life.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yea, we may even say thus, “I have received bread, I will give Bread; I have received drink, I will give Drink; I have received houseroom, I will give a House; I was visited in sickness, I will give Health; I was visited in prison, I will give Liberty.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The bread which ye gave to My poor is consumed; the Bread which I will give both recruiteth the failing and doth not fail.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: May He then give us Bread, He who is the living Bread which came down from heaven.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When He shall give Bread, He will give Himself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what didst thou intend when thou didst lend on usury?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To give money, and to receive money; but to give a smaller sum, and to receive a larger.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “I,” saith God, “will give thee an exchange for the better for all that thou hast given Me.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if thou wert to give a pound of silver, and to receive a pound of gold, with how great joy wouldest thou be possessed?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Examine and question avarice.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “I have given a pound of silver, I receive a pound of gold!”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What proportion is there between silver and gold!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Much more then, what proportion is there between earth and heaven!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And thy silver and gold thou wert to leave here below; whereas thou wilt not abide thyself for ever here.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “And I will give thee something else, and I will give thee something more, and I will give thee something better; I will give thee even that which will last for ever.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then, Brethren, be our avarice restrained, that another, which is holy, may be enkindled.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Evil altogether is her counsel, who hinders you from doing good.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye are willing to serve an evil mistress, not owning a Good Lord.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And sometimes two mistresses occupy the heart, and tear the slave asunder who deserves to be in slavery to such a double yoke.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yes, sometimes two opposing mistresses have possession of a man, avarice and luxuriousness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Avarice says, “Keep;” luxuriousness, says, “Spend.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Under two mistresses bidding and exacting diverse things what canst thou do?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They have both their mode of address.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And when thou dost begin to be unwilling to obey them, and to take a step towards thy liberty; because they have no power to command, they use caresses.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And their caresses are more to be guarded against than their commands.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What says avarice?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Keep for thyself, keep for thy children.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thou shouldest be in want, no one will give to thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Live not for the time present only; consult for the future.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: On the other hand is luxuriousness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Live whilst thou mayest.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do good to thine own soul.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Die thou must, and thou knowest not when; thou knowest not to whom thou shalt leave what thou hast, or who shall possess it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou art taking the bread out of thine own mouth, and perhaps after thy death thine heir will not so much as place a cup of wine upon thy tomb; or if so be he place a cup, he will drink himself drunk with it, not a drop will come down to thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do well therefore to thine own soul, when and whilst thou canst.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thus avarice did enjoin one thing; “Keep for thyself, consult for the future.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Luxuriousness another, “Do well to thine own soul.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But O free man, called unto liberty, be weary, be weary of thy servitude to such mistresses as these.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Acknowledge thy Redeemer, thy Deliverer.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Serve Him, He enjoineth easier things, He enjoineth not things contrary one to another.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I am bold further to say; avarice and luxuriousness did enjoin upon thee contrary things, so that thou couldest not obey them both; and one said, “Keep for thyself, and consult for the future;” the other said, “Spend freely, do well to thine own soul.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now let thy Lord and thy Redeemer come forth, and He shall say the same, and yet no contrary things.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thou wilt not, His house hath no need of an unwilling servant.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Consider thy Redeemer, consider thy Ransom.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He came to redeem thee, He shed His Blood.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Dear He held thee whom He purchased at so dear a price.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou dost acknowledge Him who bought thee, consider from what He redeemeth thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I say nothing of the other sins which lord it proudly over thee; for thou wast serving innumerable masters.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I speak only of these two, luxuriousness and avarice, giving thee contrary injunctions, hurrying thee into different things.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Deliver thyself from them, come to thy God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thou wast the servant of iniquity, be now the servant of righteousness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The words which they spake to thee, and the contrary injunctions they gave thee, the very same thou hearest now from thy Lord, yet are His injunctions not contrary.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He doth not take away their words, but he taketh away their power.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What did avarice say to thee?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Keep for thyself, consult for the future.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The word is not changed, but the man is changed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now, if thou wilt, compare the counsellors.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The one is avarice, the other righteousness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Examine these contrary injunctions.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Keep for thyself,” says avarice.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Suppose thou art willing to obey her, ask her where thou art to keep?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Some well-defended place she will show thee, walled chamber, or iron chest.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Well, use all precautions; yet peradventure some thief in the house will burst open the secret places; and whilst thou art taking precautions for thy money, thou wilt be in fear of thy life.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It may be whilst thou art keeping up thy store, he whose mind is set to plunder them, has it even in his thoughts to kill thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lastly, even though by various precautions thou shouldest defend thy treasure and thy clothes against thieves; defend them still against the rust and moth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What canst thou do then?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Here is no enemy without to take away thy goods, but one within consuming them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: No good counsel then has avarice given.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See she has enjoined thee to keep, yet has not found a place where thou mayest keep.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let her give also her next advice, “Consult for the future.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what future? for a few and those uncertain days.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: She says, “Consult for the future,” to a man who, it may be, will not live even till to-morrow.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But suppose him to live as long as avarice thinks he will, not as long as she can prove, or assure him, or have any confidence about, but suppose him to live as long as she thinks, that he grow old and so come to his end: when he is even now bent double with old age, and leaning on his stick for support, still is he seeking gain, and hears avarice saying still, “Consult for the future.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what future?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When he is even at his last breath she speaks.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: She says, “for thy children’s sake.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Would that at least we did not find the old men who had no children avaricious.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yet to these even, to such as these even, who cannot even excuse their iniquity by any empty show of natural affection, she ceases not to say, “Consult for the future.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But it may be that these will soon blush for themselves; so let us look to those who have children, whether they are certain that their children will possess what they shall leave?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let them observe in their lifetime the children of other men, some losing what they had by the unjust violence of others, others by their own wickedness consuming what they possessed; and they remain in poor estate, who were the children of rich men.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Cease then to be the home-born slaves of avarice.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But a man will say, “My children will possess this.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is uncertain; I do not say, it is false, but at best, it is uncertain.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But now suppose it to be certain, what dost thou wish to leave them?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What thou hast gotten for thyself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Assuredly what thou hast gotten was not left thee, yet thou hast it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thou hast been able to get possession of what was not left to thee, then will they also be able to get what thou shalt not leave to them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thus have the counsels of avarice been refuted; but now let the Lord say the same words, now let righteousness speak: the words will be the same, but not the same the meaning.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Keep for thyself,” saith the Lord, “consult for the future.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now ask Him, “Where shall I keep?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Thou shalt have treasure in heaven, where no thief approacheth, nor moth corrupteth.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Against what an enduring future shalt thou keep it!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Come, ye blessed of My Father, receive the kingdom prepared for you from the foundation of the world.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And of how many days this kingdom is, the end of the passage shows.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For after He had said of those on the left hand, “So these shall go away into everlasting burning;” of those on the right hand He saith, “but the righteous into life eternal.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This is “consulting for the future.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: A future which has no future beyond it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Those days without an end are called both “days,” and “a day.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For one when he was speaking of those days, saith, “That I may dwell in the house of the Lord for length of days.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And they are called a day, “This day have I begotten thee.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now those days are one day; because there is no time, in it; that day is neither preceded by a yesterday, nor succeeded by a to-morrow.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then let us “consult for the future:” the words indeed which avarice said to thee are not different in terms from this, yet by them is avarice overthrown.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: One thing may yet be said, “But what am I to do about my children?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear on this point also the counsel of thy Lord.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thy Lord should say to thee, “The thoughts of them concern Me more who did create, than thee who didst beget them,” peradventure thou couldest have nothing to say.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yet thou wilt look upon that rich man who went away sorrowful, and was rebuked in the Gospel, and wilt say to thyself perhaps, “That rich man did evil in not selling all and giving to the poor, because he had no children; but I have children; I have those for whom I should be keeping something.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In this weakness too the Lord is ready to advise with thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I would be bold to speak through His mercy; I would be bold to say something, not of mine own imagining, but of His pity.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Keep then for thy children too, but hear me.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Suppose (such is man’s condition) any one should lose one of his children; mark, Brethren, mark how that avarice has no excuse, either as respects this world or the world to come.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Such, I say, is man’s condition; for it is not that I wish it, but we see instances.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Some Christian child has been lost: thou hast lost a Christian child; not that thou hast indeed lost him, but hast sent him before thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For he is not gone quite away, but gone before.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ask thine own faith: surely thou too wilt go thither presently, where he hath gone before.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is but a short question I ask, which yet I suppose no one will answer.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Does thy son live?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ask thy faith.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If he live then, why is his portion seized upon by his brothers?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But thou wilt say, What, will he return and possess it?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let it then be sent to him whither he is gone before; he cannot come to his goods, his goods can go to him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Consider only with Whom he is.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If any son were serving at the Court, and became the Emperor’s friend, and were to say to thee, “Sell my portion, which is there, and send it to me;” wouldest thou find what to answer him?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Well, thy son is now with the Emperor of all emperors, with the King of all kings, with the Lord of all lords; send to Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I do not say thy son is in need himself; but his Lord with whom he is, is in need upon the earth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He vouchsafes to receive here, what He gives in heaven.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do what some avaricious men are wont to do, make out a conveyance, bestow upon those who are in pilgrimage, what thou mayest receive in thine own country.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But now I am not speaking at all of thyself, but of thy child.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou art hesitating to give what is thine own, yea, rather art hesitating to restore what is another’s; surely thou art hereby convicted, that it was not for thy children that thou wast laying up.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See, thou dost not give to thy children, seeing thou wilt even take away from thy children.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: From this child at all events wilt thou take away.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why is he unworthy to receive his part, because he is living with One worthier than all?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There would be reason in it, if he with whom thy son is living, were unwilling to receive it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Rich shalt thou now be for thine house, but that the house of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So far it is then from me to say to thee, “Give what thou hast;” that I am saying to thee, “Pay that thou owest.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But thou wilt say, “His brothers will have it.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: O evil maxim, which may teach thy children to wish for their brother’s death.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If they shall be enriched by the property of their deceased brother, take heed how they may watch for one another in thine house.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then wilt thou do?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wilt thou divide his patrimony, and so give lessons of parricide?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But I am unwilling to speak of the loss of a child, lest I seem to threaten calamities, which do befall men.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us speak in some more happy and auspicious tone.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I do not say then, thou wilt have one less; reckon rather that thou hast one more.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Give Christ a place with thy children, be thy Lord added to thy family; be thy Creator added to thy offspring, be thy Brother added to the number of thy children.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For though there is so great a distance, yet hath He condescended to be a Brother.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And though He be the Father’s Only Son, He hath vouchsafed to have coheirs.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo, how bountifully hath He given! why wilt thou give in such barren sort?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou hast two children; reckon Him a third: thou hast three, let Him be reckoned as a fourth: thou hast five, let Him be called a sixth; thou hast ten, let Him be the eleventh.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I will say no more; keep the place of one child for thy Lord.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what thou shalt give to thy Lord, will profit both thee and thy children; whereas, what thou dost keep for thy children wrongly, will hurt both thee and them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now thou wilt give one portion, which thou hast reckoned as one child’s portion.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Reckon that thou hast got one child more.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What great demand is this, my Brethren?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I give you counsel only; do I use violence?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As saith the Apostle, “This I speak for your own profit, not that I may cast a snare upon you.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I imagine, Brethren, that it is a light and easy thought for a father of children to suppose that he has one child more, and thereby to procure such an inheritance as thou mayest possess for ever, both thou and thy children.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Avarice can say nothing against it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye have cried out in acclamation at these words.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Turn your words rather against her; let her not overcome you; let her not have greater power in your hearts, than your Redeemer.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let her not have greater power in your hearts, than He who exhorteth us to “lift up our hearts.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And so now let us dismiss her.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What says luxuriousness?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Do well to thine own soul.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See also the Lord says the same, “Do well to thine own soul.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What luxuriousness was saying to thee, the same saith Righteousness to thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But consider here again in what sense the words are used.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thou wouldest do well to thine own soul, consider that rich man who wished to do well to his soul, after the counsel of luxuriousness and avarice.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: His “ground brought forth plentifully, and he had no room where to bestow his fruits; and he said, What shall I do?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I have no room where to bestow my fruits; I have found out what to do; “I will pull down my” old “barns, and build new,” and will fill them, “and say to my soul, Thou hast much goods; take thy pleasure.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear the counsel against luxuriousness; “Thou fool, this night thy soul shall be required of thee; and whose shall those things be which thou hast provided?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And whither must that soul which shall be required of him go?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This night it shall be required, and shall go he knows not whither.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Consider that other luxurious, proud, rich man.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He “feasted sumptuously every day, and was clothed in purple and fine linen;” and “the poor man laid at his gate full of sores, and desired” in vain “the crumbs from the rich man’s table;” he fed the dogs with his sores, but he was not fed by the rich man.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They both died; one of them was buried; of the other what is said?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “He was carried by the Angels into Abraham’s bosom.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The rich man sees the poor man; yea rather it is now the poor man sees the rich; he longs for a drop of water on his tongue from his finger, from him who once longed for a crumb from his table.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Indeed their lot was changed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The dead rich man asks for this in vain: O let not us who are alive hear it in vain.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For he wished to return again to the world, and was not permitted; he wished one of the dead to be sent to his brethren, neither was this granted him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But what was said to him?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “They have Moses and the Prophets;” and he said, “They will not hear except one go from the dead.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Abraham said to him, “If they hear not Moses and the Prophets, neither will they believe though one go from the dead.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What luxuriousness then said in a perverted sense concerning the giving of alms, and procuring rest for our souls against the time to come, that so we may “do well to our souls,” Moses also and the Prophets have spoken.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us give ear while we are alive.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because there he will desire in vain to hear, who has despised these words when he heard them here.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Are we expecting that one should rise even from the dead, and tell us to do well to our own souls?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It has been done already: thy father hath not risen again, but thy Lord hath risen.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear Him, and accept good counsel.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Spare not thy treasures, spend as freely as thou canst.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This was the voice of luxuriousness: it has become the Lord’s Voice.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Spend as freely as thou canst, do well to thy soul, lest this night thy soul be required.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Here then ye have in Christ’s Name a discourse as I think on the duty of almsgiving.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This your voice now applauding, is then only well-pleasing to the Lord, if He see withal your hands active in works of mercy.
